name: Frontegg User Management API
what_it_does: Full-stack user management platform with REST API for B2B SaaS — providing authentication, SSO, multi-tenancy, RBAC, audit logs, feature flags, and self-service admin portal that embeds into products.
use_cases: Managing user accounts and organizations with full multi-tenant user management API
Configuring enterprise SSO per customer organization programmatically
Querying audit log events for user activity monitoring in compliance workflows
Managing role-based permissions and entitlements across tenant organizations
Embedding self-service admin portal into SaaS product for customer IT teams
not_for: Consumer-facing apps without B2B multi-tenancy needs
Simple single-tenant auth (use Auth0 or Firebase Auth)
Identity providers (Frontegg consumes IdPs, not replaces them for enterprise)
best_when: A B2B SaaS product needs a complete user management system with multi-tenancy, SSO, RBAC, and audit logs — beyond just authentication.
avoid_when: You need lightweight auth only — Frontegg includes many features that add cost and complexity if unused.

auth.notes: Client credentials OAuth2 for backend API access. Frontend SDKs for embedded UI components. Vendor API key for management operations. Tenant-scoped tokens for per-organization access.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free: up to 7,500 MAUs, basic auth features
pricing.paid_tiers: Starter from $99/month; Growth from $249/month; Enterprise custom
pricing.notes: MAU-based pricing. Feature gating — SSO, audit logs, SCIM require higher tiers. More feature-complete than WorkOS for self-service admin portal. Can get expensive at scale.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits documented per API type; management API more restrictive

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Comprehensive documentation with OpenAPI reference. Multiple SDK options. Error responses include code and message. Webhook delivery for auth events, SSO changes, and user lifecycle events.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: User creation is idempotent by email per tenant. Tenant creation idempotent by external ID. Role assignment operations are idempotent.
known_agent_gotchas: Dual token system (vendor token vs user token) — ensure correct token type for operation
Tenant (organization) context required for most user management operations
SSO configuration requires IT admin action at customer end — not fully automatable
Audit log retention limits vary by plan — archive before querying old events

security_score_components.security_notes: SOC2 Type II, ISO 27001, GDPR. OAuth2 for API access. TLS enforced. Comprehensive audit logging. EU data residency. SCIM for enterprise directory sync.
